title: After-Action Review — March 3, 2026
scope: wVL Mode: Tie Support, Box Score Parity, and /stats Wiring. Three distinct gaps existed between the wVL (Women's Viperball League) experience and the rest of the sim.

rationale.item: Draws were silently corrupted into double losses: when two teams scored equal points, both record_result calls evaluated won=False; both teams recorded a loss, neither got a point. Table points, standings, streak tracking, and div record were all wrong.

The wVL is a soccer-style pyramid with promotion and relegation — draws are a normal, common result (roughly 20–25% of professional soccer fixtures). Mis-counting them as losses could push a club into a relegation zone it didn't deserve to be in, or deny a promotion to a team that earned it via draws.

wVL box scores were abbreviated or blank: Offense/Defense/Kicking tabs were silently blank for fast-simmed games with no indication this was a fast-sim limitation rather than a bug; no quarterly scoring breakdown; /stats wvl/game.html missing Play Distribution and Plays Per Quarter sections; draw scores got score-loss styling on both teams.
Recent engineer work on the financial dashboard wasn't surfaced on /stats: none of the owner-mode financial data reached the /stats/wvl/{id}/team/{key} page for the owner's club.
references: previous engineer's financial dashboard work (get_owner_team_summary, dynasty financial_history, InvestmentAllocation)

actions.description: Full tie support in the engine: added ties/div_ties fields and a points property (3*wins + ties) to ProTeamRecord; pct now weights draws as half a win; record_result() signature changed from won: bool to won: Optional[bool] with won=None meaning draw; "T" valid in streak_type/last_5; sim_week() detects ties; get_standings() sorts by points and exposes ties/points/W-L-T div_record; get_team_detail() exposes ties/points and tags drawn games won=None.
Standings template: replaced single W-L column with PTS/W/L/T columns; last_5 loop assigns .t (amber) for draws; both ranked-tier and division sub-tables updated.
Added global CSS classes .t { color: var(--yellow); } and .score-tie { color: var(--yellow); font-weight: bold; }.

Game template: three-way win/loss/tie score conditionals; center label reads DRAW instead of FINAL when scores are equal; ported Play Distribution and Plays Per Quarter sections from college/game.html, guarded to appear only when data exists.
Schedule template: score cells in both schedule formats updated to three-way win/loss/draw CSS; intermediary as_/hs_ variables extracted to avoid evaluating g.get(...) twice.

Team template: record metric card shows W-L-T when ties > 0; schedule result column shows D (amber) for draws via three-way conditional on won_val; new Club Finances section (owner club only) showing bankroll, owner archetype, latest season revenue/expenses, and Investment Allocation table.

Router: wvl_team route now builds a financial snapshot dict (bankroll, archetype, investment categories, season_financials from financial_history) from the dynasty object in wvl_sessions, only when is_owner_club is true; passed to template as financial.

NiceGUI box score dialog: tie-aware score display (yellow tied scores, DRAW label); new _render_quarterly_scoring() walking play-by-play to compute per-quarter scores; new _render_drives() function and Drives tab reading box drive_summary; graceful empty-state message "Player stats not available for fast-simmed games." in offense/defense/kicking tabs.

risk_posture: No dynasty methods were modified — the data was already computed and stored by the previous engineer's work; it just needed a read path to the stats site.
For fast-simmed games without play-by-play, all quarter cells show 0 and only the final score column is meaningful — this is acceptable since the game was not tracked play-by-play.

non_actions.reason: CVL playoff brackets still force a winner by adding random points on a tied score. This is correct — CVL is an American college league with a playoff bracket where ties are impossible. The ProTeamRecord changes only affect ProLeagueSeason, not the CVL Season class.
The existing forum export function was left as-is. It reads team stats, not quarterly data, so it doesn't need the play_by_play walk.

The engine's promotion/relegation logic already uses get_standings() output for ranking — it reads position from the ranked list, which is now ordered by points. No additional change was needed there.

residual_risks: Streak label for draws: the streak display shows T3 for a three-game unbeaten-draw run. Technically correct but unusual; some leagues might prefer U3 (unbeaten). Not changed since it would require a template-level style decision.
PCT column for soccer: in real soccer tables PCT isn't shown at all. The PCT column is harmless but could be replaced with a goal-difference-only tiebreaker display in a future pass.

Fast-sim quarterly scores show all zeros: zero-filled quarters could confuse a user who doesn't know what fast-sim means. A label like "( fast-sim — quarterly breakdown unavailable )" could be added conditionally.

Financial history on /stats requires a saved dynasty: if financial_history is empty the Club Finances section is suppressed. Correct behavior, but a note like "No financial history yet" could improve clarity.
section_headers_original: wVL Mode: Tie Support, Box Score Parity, and /stats Wiring
1. Full Tie Support in the Engine (`engine/pro_league.py`)
unmapped_content:
Branch: claude/improve-wvl-mode-Ffp0p. Files Modified: engine/pro_league.py, nicegui_app/pages/pro_leagues.py, stats_site/router.py, stats_site/templates/base.html, stats_site/templates/wvl/season.html, stats_site/templates/wvl/game.html, stats_site/templates/wvl/schedule.html, stats_site/templates/wvl/team.html.

Implementation note: Jinja2 `== true` and `== false` are used rather than Python `is True` / `is False` because Jinja2 evaluates `None == false` as truthy without the explicit equality check.

_render_quarterly_scoring uses the same algorithm used in engine/box_score.py; renders a compact 6-column table (Team | Q1 | Q2 | Q3 | Q4 | F) placed above the tab bar in every box score dialog. The tab bar now has six tabs: Team Stats, Offense, Defense, Kicking, Drives, Forum Export.
parser_notes: Date from first heading. No validation/testing section present in the document.
